title: Continuous Ingestion, Reindexing, Cutover, and Disaster Recovery · Program Brief: Rag Connectors Change Capture
summary: A nightly job rebuilds the live index in place, loses failed documents, publishes partial results, misses deletes, and has backups that were never restored. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the connectors change capture increment in recoverable continuous-indexing system. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Capture creates, updates, moves, permissions, supersession, deletes, checkpoints, source snapshots, and out-of-order events from connectors.
Test realistic query mixes, cold and warm state, filter selectivity, update load, provider limits, degraded dependencies, quality, recovery, and cost together.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example RAG engineering team handles case 2337 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to operate change capture, retries, dead letters, reindexing, blue-green cutover, deletion, and restore; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Capture creates, updates, moves, permissions, supersession, deletes, checkpoints, source snapshots, and out-of-order events from connectors. A defensible solution applies Rag Connectors Change Capture, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to recoverable continuous-indexing system.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-ingestion-operations-recovery-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-ingestion-operations-recovery-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_ingestion_operations_recovery_4(source_version: int = 14, indexed_version: int = 14, failed_documents: int = 0, dead_letters: int = 0, blue_green_ready: bool = True, restore_verified: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if source_version < 1 or indexed_version < 1:
        return False, 0, "version-input"
    if indexed_version != source_version:
        return False, indexed_version, "index-lag"
    if failed_documents != dead_letters:
        return False, failed_documents, "failure-accounting"
    if not blue_green_ready:
        return False, indexed_version, "unsafe-cutover"
    if not restore_verified:
        return False, indexed_version, "unproven-recovery"
    assert failed_documents == dead_letters
    return True, indexed_version, "recoverable-index"
